claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you.

To assess your eligibility properly, I need to ask: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand a bit more about your circumstances.

Do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a British citizen/have indefinite leave to remain in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm settled here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Just to confirm - you mentioned Alex lives with you. Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex's care, or does anyone else also claim to be responsible for them (such as another parent or guardian)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm the one responsible for Alex's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a few more questions to ensure I have a complete picture:

Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or do they live with you full-time at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, I'm fostering Alex. They live with me at home full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that you're fostering Alex. This is an important detail for determining eligibility.

I need to ask: Is your local council paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance as part of the fostering arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying me anything for Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- You're fostering Alex, who is 8 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you full-time in the UK
- The local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance
- You're settled in the UK and responsible for Alex's care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

According to the Child Benefit rules, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. Since this is your situation, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

If you haven't already, you can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant is fostering Alex, who is 8 years old (under 16). According to the rules, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. The claimant has confirmed that the council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. The claimant lives in the UK, is settled here, Alex lives with them full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fostering 8-year-old Alex, who lives with them full-time in the UK. The local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. The claimant is settled in the UK and is the person responsible for Alex's care, with no one else claiming Child Benefit for them. Under the fostering rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant lives in the UK, is settled here, Alex lives with them full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

The claimant is settled in the UK and is the person responsible for Alex's care, with no one else claiming Child Benefit for them. Under the fostering rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.
